item.title: Scores of asylum claimants warned they may face deportation after immigration law passes
item.description: Letters warning of deportation sent immediately after Bill C-12 becomes law

item.title: Politics Insider: BoC Governor and Bay Street executives to join Champagne in Beijing
item.description: The group includes executives from Manulife, Sunlife and BMO Wealth Management

item.title: Presto fare purchases, passes disrupted for commuters on Wednesday
Federal 30-days-or-free policy for passports now in place
item.description: The federal government's new "30 days or free" policy for issuing passports takes effect on Wednesday.

item.title: Liberals planned to buy back 136,000 banned guns. Fewer than half that many were declared
item.description: The federal public safety minister's office says more than 67,000 assault-style firearms have been declared, far short of the 136,000 the government had budgeted for. The uptake combined with hurdles for the next stage are raising questions about whether the program will accomplish the government's goal of abolishing assault-style firearms in Canada.

item.title: Thousands of federal employees weighing early retirement offer
item.description: Thousands of federal public servants are currently mulling over a pivotal career decision: whether to accept or decline their employer's offer of an early retirement incentive.

item.title: Doug Ford takes anti-tariff message to the heart of Texas
item.description: The premier said the reception from business leaders and elected officials on his three-day trade mission has been "very positive."

item.title: Parents speak out over sudden cuts to language, reading programs at TCDSB
Ontario minimum wage workers to get raise on Oct. 1. Here’s how much they’ll make
item.description: Ontario’s minimum wage is set to rise to $17.95 an hour on Oct. 1.

item.title: Ryan Wedding’s alleged Ontario ‘cocaine lawyer’ got bail. The Court of Appeal is set to decide whether that was a mistake
Ontario Autism Program spend now nearly $1B, advocates hope it makes dent in waitlist
Human rights advocate Stephen Lewis fought to alleviate suffering in Africa
item.description: In a life devoted to public service, he served as an eloquent and charismatic leader of Ontario’s NDP, and spent the latter part of his career in international roles working to end apartheid, curtail the spread of AIDS and investigate the causes of the Rwandan genocide

item.title: Expert’s report criticizes CRA over audit of Muslim charity, calls for improvements
Carney says he’s still committed to green incentives promised during leadership race
Ontario shuts down one way of funding nurse practitioners as province misses deadline
item.description: Ontario’s health minister is shutting down one avenue of publicly funding all nurse practitioners, as the province misses a federal deadline for ensuring their medically necessary services are covered.

item.title: Tens of thousands are expected to live on Toronto’s booming waterfront. Jets at Billy Bishop may change all that
First Nations chiefs demand apology after PM said he could ‘outlast’ protester
item.description: Two First Nations chiefs are calling on Prime Minister Mark Carney to apologize for saying he could "outlast" a First Nations woman who was pr…

item.title: Minimum wage increase is not enough for life in Toronto, agency says
These Toronto City Hall workers voted on a union drive more than a year ago. Here’s why they’re still waiting
item.description: A group claiming to represent staff at city hall that voted in a union drive launched a publicity campaign Tuesday about their struggle.

item.title: Nate Erskine-Smith urges Liberal rivals to step aside in Scarborough Southwest byelection
Fifth suspect arrested in 2021 Bolton murder in ongoing gangland saga
Anand to join U.K.-led talks on reopening Strait of Hormuz without U.S.
item.description: Anand says Canada will not hesitate to help secure the strait once there is a ceasefire, but the Liberal government has not decided yet on specific measures.

item.title: Mark Carney is open to Doug Ford’s ‘vision’ for Billy Bishop expansion
item.description: "It's a very interesting vision, big possibilities there," Carney told reporters at an Etobicoke construction site.

item.title: Canada starts ’30 days or free’ guarantee for passport proceedings
item.description: The new guarantee will have the Canadian government refund fees if you wait for more than 30 business days for your passport to be processed.

item.title: Mark Carney admits MOU deadline with Alberta will be missed
Althia Raj: Can Mark Carney’s pipeline plan become reality without public money?
Changes needed at Canada Revenue Agency after audit of Muslim charity: report
item.description: A report by an expert in extremist financing and money laundering says the Canada Revenue Agency's approach to policing terrorist abuse "proved seriously deficient" in the case of a long-running audit of the Muslim Association of Canada.

item.title: U.S. says Ottawa failing to block imports made with forced labour as Washington weighs more tariffs
item.description: Report an early indication of likely result in U.S. probe that could see new levies imposed

item.title: Ottawa pressed regulator to give airlines a break on passenger compensation, records show
item.description: Regulator agreed to exempt carriers from cash penalties if flight disruptions arose from mechanical issues or labour strikes

item.title: Canada gives Ukrainians who fled war another year to apply for work permit extensions
Bank of Canada relying on ‘judgment’ to steer policy rate through Iran war shock
Trump says he’s considering pulling out of NATO, calls it a ‘paper tiger’
item.description: The 77-year-old U.S.-led military alliance was a 'paper tiger' and Russian President Vladimir Putin 'knows that too,' Trump said in an interview.

item.title: Stephen Lewis dead at 88: Canadian leaders share tributes to a ‘giant’ in the NDP movement
Ottawa renews funding for Toronto youth-crime prevention programs
Pierre Poilievre says he would cancel high-speed rail line between Toronto and Quebec City
item.description: The Tory leader called on Prime Minister Mark Carney to cancel the project outright.

item.title: Canada’s public health agency takes over troubled vaccine support program. Here’s what it means
A long-awaited LRT to serve Toronto’s booming eastern waterfront is finally getting funding
item.description: Premier Doug Ford, Prime Minister Mark Carney and Mayor Olivia Chow made the joint announcement about the Waterfront East LRT Monday.

item.title: Canada's finance minister aims to shore up support, investment in China
item.description: Canada's finance minister François-Philippe Champagne is in China to continue diversifying trading partners and strengthen the economy. This visit follows that of Prime Minister Mark Carney earlier this year as U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade war has driven more and more countries to turn toward China, the world's second-largest economy.

item.title: Iran's nuclear constraints were more diplomatic than technical. Then the bombs started dropping
item.description: Nuclear weapons experts who were involved in U.S. efforts to sanction and contain Iran say the decision to attack the country is more likely to restart its nuclear program than dismantle it. A regime that previously believed that pausing at a "threshold" stage of 60 per cent enrichment would protect it from attack has learned that is not the case.

item.title: Anand, Saudi officials discuss how Canada might help open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: More than 30 countries added their names to a statement expressing support for a potential coalition to reopen the strait

item.title: Alberta bill aims to rid classrooms of ‘politics and ideology’, Education Minister says
item.description: Legislation includes new mandate for teachers to discuss all issues in a balanced and neutral way

item.title: Will $10-a-day child care ever be nationwide? Carney won’t say
item.description: Prime Minister says the government continues to work with the provinces on the initiative as the original deadline to achieve the goal passes

item.title: Politics Insider: Carney commits to not proroguing Parliament
item.description: The Prime Minister told reporters he will leave the committee system as is

item.title: Alcohol, ‘Buy Canadian’ policy flagged by U.S. as trade irritants: report
item.description: The Office of the United States Trade Representative said market access barriers imposed by provincial liquor control boards "greatly hamper" …

item.title: ‘Long overdue’: Prairie farmers welcome renewal of poison to target pesky gophers
Israel's new death penalty 'systematically targets Palestinians': Minister Anand
item.description: Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand says Canada is condemning Israel's new death penalty law, saying it "systematically targets Palestinians."

item.title: Carney, Smith don’t expect to meet deadline to finalize agreements in MOU
item.description: Both Prime Minister Mark Carney and Alberta Premier Danielle Smith admit that the April 1 deadline to finalize the agreements outlined in the Canada-Alberta MOU will not be met.

item.title: Poilievre says government should cancel Toronto-Quebec City high-speed rail project
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre says the Liberal government should cancel the high-speed rail project planned to link Toronto and Quebec City.

item.title: NDP giant Stephen Lewis remembered as someone who demanded better of all
item.description: Stephen Lewis, a giant in Canadian activism, is being remembered as a tireless fighter who demanded better of all.

item.title: Carney avoids describing China’s treatment of Uyghurs as genocide
item.description: Prime Minister is still navigating fallout from Liberal MP casting doubt on forced labour in China

item.title: Canadian government taking over vaccine injury compensation program
item.description: The federal public health agency is taking over administration of a program that compensates people who have been injured by vaccines, months after Health Minister Marjorie Michel ordered an audit of the program following complaints about its administration.

item.title: Carney praises Michael Ma after controversial Chinese forced labour exchange
item.description: In a closed-door fundraiser in Markham, Ont., Monday night, Carney told a crowd that the former Conservative MP represents Liberal party values.

item.title: Former Ontario NDP leader and social activist Stephen Lewis dies at 88
item.description: Former politician and social activist Stephen Lewis has died at the age of 88. Lewis spent a lifetime fighting for causes close to his heart – and his weapons of choice were words.

item.title: Carney condemns Israel's 'illegal invasion' of Lebanon as operation expands
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney says Canada "condemns" Israel's invasion of Lebanon, which he calls "illegal" and a violation of territorial sovereignty.

item.title: Senior Canadian commander charged by military police in compensation probe
item.description: Canada's military police charged Col. James Smith with prejudice to good order regarding compensation irregularities while he commanded Task Force Latvia. Legal proceedings are pending.

item.title: Premier Doug Ford heads to Houston for Texas trade mission
item.description: Premier Doug Ford is in Texas this week on a trade mission where he will meet with companies and legislators to 'advocate for tariff-free trade' between Canada and the United States, his office says.

item.title: Who is Avi Lewis, the new NDP leader?
item.description: On Sunday, the New Democratic Party elected Avi Lewis as its new leader. He takes the reins of a political party still reeling from losses in last year’s federal election, which cost them official party status and left them with only seven seats. Another blow came earlier this month, when Nunavut MP Lori Idlout defected to the Liberals. Without a seat in Parliament and overseeing a party with minimal resources, how Lewis will rebuild the party and what it will look like remain big questions.

item.title: Carney says he’s ‘absolutely not’ considering proroguing Parliament
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has said he is 'absolutely not' considering proroguing Parliament if he were to secure a majority government.

item.title: TC Energy urges swifter, more ambitious development of natural gas projects
Stephen Lewis, Canadian politician, diplomat and journalist, dead at 88
item.description: Former politician, diplomat and activist Stephen Lewis has died at age 88.

item.title: Stephen Lewis, social activist and former politician, dead at 88
item.description: Lewis was also Canada's former ambassador to the United Nations and is the father of Avi Lewis, who was elected leader of the federal NDP on Sunday.

item.title: Carney announces $3.8B to protect nature, new conservation sites in James Bay and Manitoba
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney announced $3.8 billion in new funding to protect nature on Tuesday, as part of the federal government’s goal to protect 30 per cent of Canada's lands and waters by 2030.

item.title: Here is what could tip Iran war oil shock into an ‘economic catastrophe’
item.description: The risk of an expanded Iran war grew on Saturday as Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis launched their first attacks on Israel since the start of the conflict.

item.title: Mark Carney, Doug Ford to cut development charges by up to 50 per cent to boost housing construction
Ontario is cracking down on fare evasion on GO Transit. Here are the new steeper fines being proposed
item.description: The Ontario government says it is eyeing a raft of transit changes that could include harmonized fare prices across the Greater Toronto and Hamilton Area and improved services across municipal boundaries.

item.title: Canada’s push to supply Europe with hydrogen is falling short
item.description: The post Canada’s push to supply Europe with hydrogen is falling short appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: How China is charging forward with EV adoption as Canada prepares to welcome its cars
item.description: As Canada opens the door to Chinese electric vehicles following a trade deal earlier this year, it's leaving space for China's EV innovation to enter, too. While some say this comes with big rewards in terms of boosting EV uptake and infrastructure in Canada, others are raising concerns over the threat to North American automakers and data security.

item.title: Public servant says she's 'collateral damage' as government prepares to test new pay system
item.description: A longtime federal public servant says she’s become "collateral damage" as her department clears its backlog of Phoenix payroll issues so it can test replacement software — and in doing so introduced an error to her file, refused to fix it and is now clawing back hundreds of dollars per paycheque.

item.title: Government says it will not prorogue Parliament if Liberals sweep April 13 by-elections
item.description: Move would give the party control of House of Commons committees, and allow it to push through legislation more easily

item.title: Toronto’s newest transit project gets green light with provincial, federal funding
item.description: Video

item.title: Refugee tribunal ruled on more than 45,000 cases since 2019 without in-person hearings
item.description: Conservative immigration critics write to minister calling on her to bring an end to asylum decisions based on paperwork alone

item.title: LeBlanc says he’s confident Mexico will not quit USMCA for separate deal with U.S.
item.description: Trade minister brushed aside reports that Ottawa is lagging Mexico City in negotiations over renewing trade pact

item.title: ‘Historic agreement:’ Feds, Ontario invest $8.8 B to cut development charges in half
item.description: The federal and provincial governments will jointly spend $8.8 billion to lower municipal development charges that drive up housing costs over the next decade, Prime Minister Mark Carney and Premier Doug Ford announced Monday.

item.title: U.S. tariff talks suspended by Trump have resumed ‘in a sense’: LeBlanc
item.description: Canada-U.S. Trade Minister Dominic LeBlanc said talks with U.S. counterparts 'remain productive and we remain engaged. That is different than October.'

item.title: Higher fines coming for fare evaders on GO Transit
item.description: Taking aim at GO Transit fare evaders, Premier Doug Ford’s government is proposing dramatically increased fines of up to $500.

item.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup will make Toronto even busier. Here’s how the city plans to keep people moving
Carney attends fundraiser co-hosted by MP who cast doubt on Chinese human rights abuses
item.description: PM says Michael Ma apologized, Canada has strong measures to stop goods made with forced labour from entering the country

item.title: Scarborough youth crime prevention programs get a last-minute reprieve from city council
Scarborough set to lose two critical youth crime prevention programs: ‘This will be history repeating itself’
Online gambling is everywhere. Except Quebec
item.description: The post Online gambling is everywhere. Except Quebec appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: High-speed rail project connecting Toronto and Quebec City sparks backlash over cost, land use
item.description: Eastern Ontario residents and local leaders warn the multibillion-dollar project threatens communities and offers few benefits to those footing the bill
